content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from Topic A's ICM-based autonomous agents) can be repurposed to prioritize which new unlabeled data samples get stored and fine-tuned into an active learning agent's long-term memory, creating a self-sustaining memory system that outperforms static SOTA 2024 active selection rules. This means 2025 SOTA gains in active learning agent memory will not come from larger storage arrays or better retrieval alone, but from tying memory retention directly to the agent's inherent curiosity signal.
